target: data/sections.json part8b — two-anchor patch to SEC-8b.3 L3a paragraph. (1) Rename sub-paragraph header from "Fail-clause tension" to "Fail-clause double-bind." (2) Replace the single-sentence Fail-clause-tension argument with a forced-disjunction (double-bind) argument that acknowledges both natural readings of the dome's Fail-clause phrase "signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone" and shows that neither reading rescues discrimination. Resolves ISS-1342 (c4 hole #1 moderate).
patch_instructions: Two edits to sections.json part8b.html. For each anchor, find the unique substring patch_anchors[i].before (verified 1 occurrence on 2026-04-22 against the workspace file) and replace with patch_anchors[i].after. No other sections.json keys are touched. The TLDR for 8b.3 is unchanged (it does not mention Fail-clause tension). WIN-068, PRED-095, and all other section prose are unchanged. Order of application does not matter — the two anchors are disjoint.

patch_anchors.before: L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause tension:
patch_anchors.after: L3a (PRED-095, magnetic) — sub-tests, goalpost scope, Fail-clause double-bind:
patch_anchors.rationale: The sub-paragraph header still labelled the argument a "tension," but under the new forced-disjunction framing there is NO tension under interpretation A (the baseline-Sq null reading). Keeping "tension" in the header mislabels the paragraph's own conclusion. "Double-bind" captures the argument structure accurately: whichever reading a dome defender picks, the Pass band fails to discriminate.

patch_anchors.before: The Fail-clause concession that “signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone” would falsify the claim is in tension with the Pass criterion: Chapman’s mechanism is the Sq-dynamo, so a confirmed signal in our codified band is consistent with both models simultaneously, and triggering the Fail-clause would require abandoning the Pass-clause’s framing of what counts as a signal.
patch_anchors.after: The Fail-clause concession that “signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone” would falsify the claim admits two natural readings, and the dome does not disambiguate between them. Read as a baseline-Sq null test (interpretation A — “the observed signal is indistinguishable from background Sq variation, without any eclipse-driven enhancement”), the Fail-clause is a fair null check that rules out dome and standard Chapman equally, because both models require an eclipse-specific enhancement; there is no tension with Pass, but this reading also concedes that a Pass in our codified band carries no dome-specific forecast, since Chapman’s mechanism is the Sq-dynamo and already predicts an eclipse-driven enhancement in the same magnitude range. Read as targeting the eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo (interpretation B — “the observed signal can be explained by the Chapman mechanism’s response to the eclipse alone, without dome input”), Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive, because the Pass criterion credits exactly that signal. The dome does not publish which reading governs, and under either reading the Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe — reading A surrenders discrimination to preserve consistency; reading B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible.
patch_anchors.rationale: c4 hole #1 recommendation option (a), adopted. The original closing sentence forced interpretation B ("Chapman-mechanism IS the Sq-dynamo, so Pass and Fail are mutually incompatible") without argument, and a dome defender can naturally read the Fail-clause phrase "signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone" as interpretation A (baseline Sq without eclipse enhancement) — a standard null-hypothesis test that rules out both dome and Chapman equally and carries no tension with Pass. Under the original sentence, interpretation A was an open escape route. The replacement names both interpretations, shows what each concedes, and concludes the non-discrimination survives on either branch: under A, no tension but also no dome-specific forecast; under B, Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive. This converts a rebuttable single-horn tension claim into a forced disjunction (double-bind) that forecloses both escape routes.

holes_addressed.hole: H1 (moderate) — ISS-1342 / SEC-8b.3 c4 hole #1 — Fail-clause "in tension" argument ambiguous between two readings
holes_addressed.description: The post-EXP-205 L3a paragraph preserves a single-horn tension claim: "signal explained by ionospheric Sq variation alone" would falsify the claim "in tension with" Pass because Chapman's mechanism IS the Sq-dynamo. The curmudgeon's c4 review flagged (correctly) that this reading forces interpretation B without argument: a dome defender can read the Fail-clause phrase as interpretation A (baseline Sq without eclipse-driven enhancement), a standard null-hypothesis test that rules out BOTH dome and Chapman equally (both require eclipse-specific enhancement), in which case there is no tension with Pass. Under interpretation A, the paragraph's closing tension claim is rebuttable and becomes a dome-defender's opening. EXP-205 explicitly flagged this as out-of-scope follow-up and the hole carried forward unchanged from c3 to c4.
holes_addressed.fix: Replace the single-sentence tension argument with a four-sentence forced-disjunction argument. Sentence 1 acknowledges the Fail-clause admits two natural readings and the dome does not disambiguate. Sentences 2–3 state interpretation A (baseline-Sq null) with its payload — no tension with Pass, but also no dome-specific forecast since Chapman already predicts an eclipse-driven enhancement in the same magnitude range — and interpretation B (eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo) with its payload: Pass and Fail become mutually exclusive. Sentence 4 closes the double-bind: under either reading the Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe (A surrenders discrimination to preserve consistency; B attempts discrimination by making Pass and Fail incompatible). Rename the sub-paragraph header from "Fail-clause tension" to "Fail-clause double-bind" so the header tracks the argument structure.
holes_addressed.inverts: Before: single-horn claim that relies on interpretation B; vulnerable to a reader who chooses interpretation A. After: forced disjunction that evaluates both natural readings and shows non-discrimination on each branch. Regardless of which reading a dome defender selects, the Pass band does not distinguish dome from globe — either no discrimination (A) or incompatible Pass/Fail (B).

new_evidence_added: No new external evidence. The patch restates the existing arithmetic and Chapman-mechanism facts already present in the surrounding SEC-8b.3 paragraphs (baseline × coverage × geometry weight shared between dome W004 recalibration and standard Chapman-Sq-dynamo; Chapman mechanism = Sq-dynamo ionospheric conductivity reduction). The novelty is argument structure only: the Fail-clause critique is recast from a rebuttable single-horn tension into a forced disjunction.

anticipated_objections.objection: Interpretation A already appears by implication in the preceding paragraph's non-discrimination argument (shared arithmetic ⇒ Pass in the W004 band is consistent with both models). Making A explicit adds redundancy.
anticipated_objections.response: The preceding paragraphs establish that a Pass in the codified band does not discriminate dome from globe (structural-arithmetic argument). They do NOT address what the Fail-clause means, only what a Pass means. The original closing sentence introduced a separate Fail-clause argument and concluded a tension; that argument only works under interpretation B, and a defender who reads the Fail-clause as interpretation A escapes the conclusion without rebutting the preceding non-discrimination paragraphs. The new sentence connects the Fail-clause explicitly to both readings and shows the Fail-clause adds nothing discriminating under either — regardless of which Pass-side argument a defender accepts. It is a closure of the argument at a place where the old text left a door open.

anticipated_objections.objection: The replacement is ~170 words; the original was ~80. This is a disproportionate expansion for a moderate hole.
anticipated_objections.response: The length is load-bearing. A forced-disjunction argument requires (a) naming both interpretations explicitly, (b) spelling out what each concedes, and (c) closing the disjunction with a shared conclusion. Any shorter form risks collapsing back into a single-horn claim (which is the hole being fixed) or a vague gesture at "ambiguity" without showing how the ambiguity is fatal in either direction. The four-sentence structure is the minimum to make the double-bind explicit and auditable. c4 hole #1 recommendation option (a) is the source form; we adopt it with light prose polish.

anticipated_objections.objection: Renaming the sub-paragraph header from "Fail-clause tension" to "Fail-clause double-bind" widens the scope beyond the single-sentence fix.
anticipated_objections.response: The header is a summary label for the paragraph's final argument. The argument no longer asserts a tension (under interpretation A there is explicitly no tension), so retaining "tension" in the header mislabels the paragraph's own conclusion and is internally inconsistent with the new body. Updating the header preserves scanability for a reader who navigates by bold labels. The edit is one substring on a short anchor; it does not touch any other content. c4 hole #1 recommendation (a) does not name the header explicitly, but the spirit of option (a) requires the label to track the argument.

anticipated_objections.objection: A dome defender may propose a third reading — e.g., "signal explained by baseline Sq PLUS eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq-dynamo, BOTH excluded, leaving only dome-specific signal."
anticipated_objections.response: That reading collapses into interpretation B for the purposes of the Pass/Fail relation. If the Fail-clause rules out both baseline Sq AND eclipse-modulated Chapman Sq, then the surviving "Pass" population is signals that cannot be produced by Chapman's mechanism at all — which requires the dome's W004 recalibration to generate a signal that Chapman's latitude-kernel choice cannot reproduce. But the structural-arithmetic argument in the preceding paragraph (baseline × coverage × geometry weight, shared corpus) shows that any signal dome produces with an O(1) FSF weighting, a Chapman treatment with a matching latitude kernel also produces. So the third reading either collapses into B (Pass and Fail mutually exclusive by the same structural-arithmetic argument) or requires a dome-specific physical signature the codified magnitude-only Pass band does not probe. It does not open a new escape route.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's request for a layered eclipse audit (fair-audit point 3) pushed our review toward the cleanest possible framing of the backward-vs-forward distinction — and SEC-8b.3 genuinely benefits from that restructuring. The Fail-clause clause, as worded, IS ambiguous; a dome defender reading it as a null test (interpretation A) is being entirely reasonable about the English. Our original tension claim quietly assumed the less charitable reading. The kernel of truth is that a careful dome defender's "you're strawmanning my Fail-clause" rebuttal is partially valid — on interpretation A, there is no tension and our original sentence overreached. The fix is to take that reading seriously, show its price (no dome-specific forecast), and extend the non-discrimination argument across both branches so the critique lands on whichever reading the defender picks. The double-bind is stronger than the original tension claim precisely because it concedes what deserves conceding.
tldr_change_note: The 8b.3 TLDR summarizes two surviving structural critiques (ensemble-anchor-is-calibration; magnitude-only Pass band doesn't discriminate). The Fail-clause argument is an embedded strand inside critique #2, not a separate named critique, so the TLDR continues to hold verbatim after the patch. No TLDR edit required.
out_of_scope_deliberately: c4 hole #2 (moderate) — globe-parasitism framing conflates input format with physical dependency; tighten closing sentence of L3a threshold-codification paragraph. Not covered by ISS-1342; scope to its own issue/EXP.

c4 hole #3 (moderate) — "two distinct dome-authored formulas" understates the inject_ai_layer.py / BOU-family relationship; reframe as "one public dual-baseline schema + one internal monitor.py KAPPA-squared variant." Not covered by ISS-1342; scope to its own issue/EXP.
c4 hole #4 (minor) — TLDR and body "Tier 3" labeling conflates our L3a audit layer with the dome's PRED-TIER-N scheme. Scope to its own issue.
c4 hole #5 (minor) — "1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse" citation-year imprecision (paper is Wang et al. 2000; 1997 refers to the Mohe eclipse event). Scope to its own issue.
c4 hole #6 (minor) — station-cluster boundary phrasing "above 80% coverage" vs HAD at exactly 0.80. Scope to its own issue.
c4 hole #7 (minor) — monitor.py −29 nT "for a prior eclipse" phrasing vague about which eclipse. Scope to its own issue.

No changes to SEC-8b.3 TLDR, to other SEC-8b.* sub-sections, or to any prose outside the two anchors identified. No changes to WIN-068, WIN-011, PRED-095, or PRED-TIER-2 — the Fail-clause framing is local to SEC-8b.3 (verified: grep for "Fail-clause" returns zero matches in WIN-068 and PRED-095).
verification_steps_run: Counted occurrences of each anchor substring in workspace data/sections.json — both unique (1 occurrence each).
Dry-run applied both patches in memory; re-serialized the sections.json; confirmed JSON.parse round-trip succeeds with no errors.

Searched part8b.html for residual "tension" occurrences post-patch: 1 remains, which is the intended "there is no tension with Pass" phrase in the new interpretation-A clause. No stale "Fail-clause tension" label survives.
Cross-checked WIN-068 and PRED-095 for Fail-clause framing: zero matches, so no cross-section propagation needed.
Confirmed 8b.3 TLDR does not mention "tension" or "Fail-clause" — no TLDR edit needed.
propagation_note: SEC-8b.3 has now been through curmudgeon cycles c2, c3, c4 with three sequential expansions (EXP-195 baseline, EXP-205 major c3-hole rewrite, EXP-224 this patch). After integration, the c4 moderate hole #1 is closed; two moderate holes (#2 globe-parasitism phrasing, #3 three-formulas framing) and four minor holes remain open and should be queued for future cycles. The paragraph's overall thesis — Pass band fails to distinguish dome from globe — is unchanged; this patch strengthens the Fail-clause strand from a rebuttable tension to a forced disjunction.
